active_attempt_resolution_states.authority_safety_posture: Manifest lease truth governs active-attempt isolation, not scheduler optimism.
active_attempt_resolution_states.operator_visible_effect: Operators can see when a client was deferred because live work already existed.
active_attempt_resolution_states.recovery_posture: Same-window active attempts resolve to defer or stale-reclaim-required posture rather than duplicate execution.
active_attempt_resolution_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly selection encounters active or stale manifest work for the same client-period tuple.

ineligible_or_deferred_conditions.authority_safety_posture: Authority ambiguity, step-up, approval, override, or active lease blocks unattended execution.
ineligible_or_deferred_conditions.operator_visible_effect: Morning handoff can explain why a client did not execute overnight.
ineligible_or_deferred_conditions.recovery_posture: Condition is persisted explicitly instead of vanishing because the window filled up or truth changed.
ineligible_or_deferred_conditions.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly selector evaluates whether a candidate can execute this window.

operator_digest_publication_states.authority_safety_posture: Digest publication must remain derived from persisted nightly artifacts and queues.
operator_digest_publication_states.operator_visible_effect: Operators can tell whether digest publication is not ready, pending workflow publication, pending notification publication, or complete.
operator_digest_publication_states.recovery_posture: Digest publication remains explicit across retries and quiescence rather than being implied by missing inbox work.
operator_digest_publication_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly batch reaches a digest-publication boundary after selection or quiescence.

quiescence_outcome_buckets.authority_safety_posture: Authority-waiting and late-data-waiting posture remain explicit rather than hidden as generic failure.
quiescence_outcome_buckets.operator_visible_effect: Morning handoff can summarize completed, deferred, review-required, blocked, failed, reused, or skipped outcomes.
quiescence_outcome_buckets.recovery_posture: Finite-progress buckets ensure overnight behavior converges to explicit quiescent posture.
quiescence_outcome_buckets.trigger_or_entry_condition: A selected entry, client execution, or batch convergence settles into one explicit bucket.

recovery_resume_states.authority_safety_posture: Stale-batch reclamation requires durable proof, not missing heartbeat alone.
recovery_resume_states.operator_visible_effect: Nightly recovery dashboards can tell whether selection and shard plans were resumed or rebuilt.
recovery_resume_states.recovery_posture: Successor batches must link the predecessor and either resume its selection/shards or explicitly reshards them.
recovery_resume_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: A batch is either a fresh window or a stale-batch recovery successor.

recovery_resume_states.trigger_or_entry_condition: A batch is either a fresh window or a stale-batch recovery successor.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Later recovery or manual retry windows may not impersonate a scheduled window.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can tell whether overnight work was scheduled, manual retry, or stale-batch reclaim.
rows.recovery_posture: Trigger class is part of the batch identity and duplicate-suppression law.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly control-plane allocation observes a new trigger window.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
rows.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
rows.state_or_outcome: Allocate a new manifest because no reusable terminal result or lawful continuation satisfies the request.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
rows.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
rows.state_or_outcome: Allocate a continuation child only when same-request reuse is unlawful and reclaim or continuation is explicitly required.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
rows.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
rows.state_or_outcome: Attach a prior terminal result before considering fresh execution.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
rows.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
rows.state_or_outcome: Active same-window attempt exists and remains live under manifest start lease.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
rows.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
rows.state_or_outcome: Retry could be legal later, but capacity, checkpoint timing, or next_retry_at defers it to a later window.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Manifest lease truth governs active-attempt isolation, not scheduler optimism.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can see when a client was deferred because live work already existed.
rows.recovery_posture: Same-window active attempts resolve to defer or stale-reclaim-required posture rather than duplicate execution.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly selection encounters active or stale manifest work for the same client-period tuple.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Manifest lease truth governs active-attempt isolation, not scheduler optimism.

rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly selection encounters active or stale manifest work for the same client-period tuple.

rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly selection encounters active or stale manifest work for the same client-period tuple.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Stale-batch reclamation requires durable proof, not missing heartbeat alone.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Nightly recovery dashboards can tell whether selection and shard plans were resumed or rebuilt.
rows.recovery_posture: Successor batches must link the predecessor and either resume its selection/shards or explicitly reshards them.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A batch is either a fresh window or a stale-batch recovery successor.

rows.authority_safety_posture: One stuck shard may not serialize unrelated shards or hide their outcomes.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operations can see whether one shard failed in isolation, blocked the tenant, or requires reclaim.
rows.recovery_posture: Shard-local failure isolation keeps unrelated clients explicit even when one shard fails.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Shard-level execution or recovery isolates one subset of selected entries.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Authority-waiting and late-data-waiting posture remain explicit rather than hidden as generic failure.

rows.recovery_posture: Finite-progress buckets ensure overnight behavior converges to explicit quiescent posture.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: A selected entry, client execution, or batch convergence settles into one explicit bucket.

rows.authority_safety_posture: Digest publication must remain derived from persisted nightly artifacts and queues.
rows.operator_visible_effect: Operators can tell whether digest publication is not ready, pending workflow publication, pending notification publication, or complete.
rows.recovery_posture: Digest publication remains explicit across retries and quiescence rather than being implied by missing inbox work.
rows.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly batch reaches a digest-publication boundary after selection or quiescence.

selection_dispositions.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.
selection_dispositions.operator_visible_effect: Nightly operations can see whether a client executed, reused, deferred, escalated, or skipped.
selection_dispositions.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
selection_dispositions.state_or_outcome: Allocate a new manifest because no reusable terminal result or lawful continuation satisfies the request.
selection_dispositions.trigger_or_entry_condition: One frozen selection entry is classified during nightly portfolio selection.

selection_dispositions.authority_safety_posture: Reuse is evaluated before new execution and live active attempts may not silently duplicate manifests.

selection_dispositions.recovery_posture: Every candidate gets one persisted selection row and preserves its disposition across reclaim and restart.
selection_dispositions.state_or_outcome: Allocate a continuation child only when same-request reuse is unlawful and reclaim or continuation is explicitly required.

trigger_classes.authority_safety_posture: Later recovery or manual retry windows may not impersonate a scheduled window.

trigger_classes.trigger_or_entry_condition: Nightly control-plane allocation observes a new trigger window.
